name: The Travel Book Shop
description: The real-life inspiration for the bookshop owned by William Thacker (Hugh Grant). The original Travel Bookshop that inspired the film was located on Blenheim Crescent and became a pilgrimage site for fans worldwide.
sceneDescription: William Thacker runs a quiet travel bookshop in Notting Hill. His ordinary life takes an extraordinary turn when the world's most famous movie star, Anna Scott, walks into his shop to browse.
sceneQuote: Can I help you at all?
visitTip: Visit early morning for the quiet Notting Hill atmosphere the film captures so well.

name: Platform 9 3/4
description: The iconic platform where Hogwarts students board the Hogwarts Express. A special photo spot has been created at King's Cross Station with a luggage trolley appearing to vanish into the wall between platforms 9 and 10.
sceneDescription: Young Harry Potter, guided by the Weasley family, runs through the solid barrier between platforms 9 and 10 at King's Cross Station to reach the magical Platform 9 3/4 and board the Hogwarts Express for the first time.
sceneQuote: All you have to do is walk straight at the barrier between platforms 9 and 10.
visitTip: The Harry Potter shop next to the platform sells wands, robes, and other merchandise.

name: Borough Market
description: Bridget Jones's flat was located above The Globe pub near Borough Market. This vibrant food market, one of London's oldest, serves as the backdrop for many scenes in the film.
sceneDescription: Bridget's flat sits above the lively Borough Market. She looks out her window at the bustling market below, contemplating her love life while penning confessions in her diary.
sceneQuote: It is a truth universally acknowledged that when one part of your life starts going okay, another falls spectacularly to pieces.
visitTip: Try the grilled cheese sandwiches at Borough Market - a local favorite since the 1200s.

name: Shakespeare's Globe Theatre
description: A faithful reconstruction of the original Globe Theatre associated with Shakespeare. While the film used studio sets, this modern recreation on the Thames South Bank brings the world of the film to life.
sceneDescription: The Rose Theatre scenes in the film echo the atmosphere of the real Globe. Will Shakespeare presents his new play 'Romeo and Juliet' to an enraptured audience, with Viola watching from the galleries.
visitTip: Book standing 'groundling' tickets for the most authentic Elizabethan theatre experience.

name: Central Perk (The Friends Building)
description: The exterior of 90 Bedford Street was used as the establishing shot for the building housing Monica's apartment and the Central Perk coffee shop. The cozy West Village corner remains a top pilgrimage for Friends fans.
sceneDescription: The six friends gather on the iconic orange couch at Central Perk, their favorite coffeehouse in Greenwich Village. Gunther serves coffee while Rachel, Monica, Phoebe, Ross, Joey, and Chandler navigate life and love.
sceneQuote: Welcome to the real world. It sucks. You're gonna love it.
visitTip: The Little Owl restaurant occupies the ground floor today and is worth a meal.

name: Empire State Building Observatory
description: The Empire State Building observation deck is where Sam and Annie finally meet in the climactic scene. The film pays homage to 'An Affair to Remember' with this iconic rooftop reunion.
sceneDescription: On Valentine's Day, Annie rushes to the top of the Empire State Building. She finds a child's backpack left behind, and as she picks it up, Sam and his son Jonah step out of the elevator. Their eyes meet at last.
sceneQuote: It was a sign... I knew it. I knew it the first time I touched her hand.
visitTip: Visit at sunset for the most romantic views - just like the film's finale.

name: Tiffany & Co. Fifth Avenue
description: The legendary Tiffany & Co. flagship store where Holly Golightly (Audrey Hepburn) has her breakfast while gazing at the window displays. One of the most iconic opening scenes in cinema history.
sceneDescription: In the early morning light, Holly Golightly steps out of a yellow taxi onto a deserted Fifth Avenue. Wearing her iconic black dress and pearls, she gazes into Tiffany's windows while eating a pastry and sipping coffee.
sceneQuote: Nothing very bad could happen to you at Tiffany's.
visitTip: Arrive early in the morning to recreate Holly's iconic moment on the quiet sidewalk.

name: Katz's Delicatessen
address: 205 E Houston Street, Lower East Side
description: The famous deli where one of cinema's most memorable scenes was filmed. A sign hanging above a specific table reads 'Where Harry met Sally... hope you have what she had!' commemorating the iconic moment.
sceneDescription: Sally demonstrates to a skeptical Harry that women can fake enjoyment at a restaurant. Her increasingly dramatic performance culminates in a nearby customer telling her waiter the legendary line.
visitTip: Ask to sit at 'the table' and order the famous pastrami on rye.

name: Cafe des Deux Moulins
description: The real cafe where Amelie Poulain works as a waitress. Located in the heart of Montmartre, this authentic Parisian cafe retains much of its film appearance and has become a must-visit for Amelie fans.
sceneDescription: Amelie works as a waitress at the Cafe des Deux Moulins, observing the quirky regulars and hatching her plans to secretly improve the lives of those around her while cracking creme brulee with a spoon.
visitTip: Order the creme brulee - a nod to Amelie's favorite pleasure.

name: Pont Neuf
description: Paris's oldest standing bridge, featured in numerous films including Amelie. The bridge spans the Seine at the tip of the Ile de la Cite and offers stunning views of the river and surrounding architecture.
sceneDescription: Amelie skips stones on the Canal Saint-Martin, but the Pont Neuf appears as she traverses Paris on her secret missions, the ancient bridge connecting her Montmartre world to the heart of the city.
sceneQuote: She is going to be a catalyst for mischief.
visitTip: Walk across at sunset for the most magical Parisian views.

name: Shakespeare and Company
description: The legendary English-language bookshop where Jesse and Celine reunite in Before Sunset. Founded in 1951, Shakespeare and Company is a literary landmark on the Left Bank facing Notre-Dame.
sceneDescription: Nine years after their magical night in Vienna, Jesse is giving a reading at Shakespeare and Company for his new book. Celine appears in the audience, and they walk out together into the Paris afternoon.
sceneQuote: I guess when you're young, you just believe there'll be many people with whom you'll connect. Later in life you realize it only happens a few times.
visitTip: Browse the book-lined rooms upstairs and enjoy the view of Notre-Dame from the bench outside.

name: Plaza de Espana
address: Av. de Isabel la Catolica, Seville
description: The majestic Plaza de Espana doubled as the city of Theed on Naboo in Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones. Its sweeping Renaissance architecture and ornamental bridges created a perfect alien cityscape.
sceneDescription: Anakin Skywalker and Padme Amidala walk through the grand corridors of the royal palace on Naboo, discussing politics and destiny as their forbidden romance begins to blossom.
sceneQuote: I don't like sand. It's coarse and rough and irritating and it gets everywhere.
visitTip: Visit the tiled alcoves representing each Spanish province - they are stunning for photos.

name: The Travel Book Shop
description: A quiet London bookshop doubles as a dead-drop for the artifact's courier. The crew uses the chaos of the morning rush to swap documents without being seen.
sceneDescription: A seemingly ordinary customer exchange triggers a chase that spills out onto Portobello Road.
sceneQuote: You were never buying a book, were you?
visitTip: Arrive early to enjoy the colorful street without crowds.

name: Pont Neuf
description: A midnight exchange on Paris's oldest bridge reveals a hidden cipher engraved on the stone balustrade.
sceneDescription: The crew meets a mysterious contact under the street lamps as the Seine shimmers below.
sceneQuote: Paris is loud at midnight—if you know what to listen for.
visitTip: Visit at sunset for dramatic lighting across the river.

name: Cafe des Deux Moulins
description: A coded message is delivered in a sugar packet at a cozy Montmartre cafe.
sceneDescription: Hidden in plain sight, the team deciphers a menu-printed map while tourists sip espresso around them.
visitTip: Grab a window seat to watch the street life of Montmartre.

name: Empire State Building Observatory
description: The artifact's trail leads to a rooftop rendezvous above Manhattan.
sceneDescription: A high-stakes pursuit erupts on the observatory deck as the skyline becomes the ultimate escape route.
sceneQuote: No room to run, only up.
visitTip: Night visits offer a cinematic view of the city lights.

name: Plaza de Espana
address: Av. de Isabel la Catolica, Seville
description: The final handoff takes place in Seville's grand plaza, where every arch hides a lookout.
sceneDescription: A sweeping crane shot follows the team as they cross the bridges and close the net.
sceneQuote: This is where the chase ends.
visitTip: Walk the tiled alcoves for a panoramic view of the plaza.
